heroTip: In China, Alipay and WeChat Pay are the dominant payment methods — link your card before you go.
bestPaymentMethod: Alipay / WeChat Pay (linked to a credit card)
bestCard: KBank Mastercard or SCB Visa — among the lowest FX fees available.
topTips: Register Alipay or WeChat Pay before departure and link your credit card.
Scan-to-pay QR codes work almost everywhere in China — carry minimal cash.
If you need cash, withdraw CNY from ATMs that support UnionPay (available at airports).
Choose a bank with a low FX fee such as KBank or SCB.
Always check the rate before swiping — never accept billing in your home currency (DCC).
avoidTip: Never accept DCC when paying by card at merchants or hotels in China.
dccNote: Many merchants in China offer to charge in your home currency — always decline and choose to pay in CNY.

fxFeeItems.name: FX Fee (Foreign Transaction Fee)
fxFeeItems.detail: Fee your bank charges for foreign card use — typically 1.5%–2.5% of the transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: Spread
fxFeeItems.detail: The gap between mid-market rate and the rate your bank actually gives — usually 0.5%–2%.

fxFeeItems.name: ATM Fee
fxFeeItems.detail: Withdrawal fees from both your home bank and the local ATM — combined can reach $5–$10 per transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: DCC Markup
fxFeeItems.detail: Extra markup when you choose to pay in your home currency — often 3%–10% above the true market rate.

attractions.name: Great Wall of China (Mutianyu)
attractions.detail: Most scenic and well-preserved section of the Great Wall with cable car access and far fewer crowds than Badaling.
attractions.tip: Foreign cards rarely work on-site — bring cash CNY. WeChat Pay and Alipay accepted at some stalls.

attractions.name: Forbidden City
attractions.detail: World's largest palace complex with 9,999 rooms and 600 years of imperial history at the heart of Beijing.
attractions.tip: Tickets must be booked online via WeChat Pay or Alipay — foreign cards are often declined.

attractions.name: Terracotta Army
attractions.detail: Over 8,000 life-size clay soldiers guarding the tomb of China's first emperor — a UNESCO World Heritage site in Xi'an.
attractions.tip: Cash CNY or local payment apps accepted. Bring a VPN for Google Maps navigation en route.

attractions.name: The Bund, Shanghai
attractions.detail: Iconic 1.5km waterfront promenade of heritage colonial buildings facing the futuristic Pudong skyline across the Huangpu River.
attractions.tip: Bund restaurants and cafes settle bills via Alipay/WeChat Pay or cash CNY — have both ready.

attractions.name: West Lake, Hangzhou
attractions.detail: Serene lake immortalized in classical Chinese poetry with pagodas, willow-lined causeways, and Dragon Well tea gardens nearby.
attractions.tip: Boat ticket booths accept cash; nearby tea houses often use QR code payment apps.
